title: 10 Time-Tested Herbal Essential Oils for Fresh Breath

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oil, also known as peppermint oil, has natural antibacterial properties that help to kill bacteria in the mouth that can cause bad breath. This oil has a strong, refreshing scent that can stimulate saliva production, which helps to wash away bacteria and food particles that can lead to bad breath. The menthol in peppermint oil also has a cooling effect on the mouth and throat, which can help to reduce inflammation and kill germs that can cause bad breath. Additionally, peppermint oil has a natural deodorizing effect, which can help to mask unpleasant odors in the mouth. When used in a toothpaste, mouthwash, or breath spray, peppermint essential oil can help to freshen the breath and leave the mouth feeling clean and refreshed.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil for bad breath, you will need fresh sage leaves, a carrier oil, and a clean glass jar. First, harvest or buy fresh sage leaves, making sure they are free from any dirt or contaminants. Then, dry the sage leaves in a cool and dark place, or use a food dehydrator to speed up the process. Next, chop the dried sage leaves into small pieces and mix them with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a ratio of about 1:3 (one part sage to three parts carrier oil). Store the mixture in a clean glass jar and let it sit for 2-3 weeks in a cool and dark place, shaking the jar every day to help the oil extract from the sage leaves. After this time, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter, and discard the sage leaves. The resulting herbal essential oil can be used to freshen breath by adding a few drops to water or mouthwash.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for bad breath

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oil is a natural remedy that can help with bad breath. Its strong antibacterial properties can kill the germs in the mouth that cause bad breath. When you use eucalyptus oil as a mouthwash or add a few drops to your toothpaste, it can help reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause bad breath. The oil's decongestant properties can also help open up the airways and reduce inflammation in the sinuses, which can help to prevent bad breath caused by sinus infections. Additionally, eucalyptus oil has a fresh and clean scent that can leave your mouth feeling and smelling fresh. The oil can also help to reduce plaque and gum inflammation, which can contribute to bad breath.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil for bad breath, start by harvesting fresh Salvia officinalis leaves from your garden or a nearby plant. Choose leaves that are free from any damage or disease, and cut them into small pieces to release their oils. Next, dry the leaves in a cool, dark place, such as a cupboard or a drying rack, until they are completely dry and crumbly. Once the leaves are dry, place them in a clean glass jar and cover them with a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil. Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day, to allow the Salvia officinalis oils to infuse into the carrier oil. After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil is now ready to use as a natural remedy for bad breath. To use it, simply apply a few drops to your tongue or gums, or mix it with water and gargle with it.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for bad breath

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass essential oil, is a natural remedy for bad breath. Its antibacterial properties help kill the germs in the mouth that cause bad breath. The oil contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have antimicrobial effects that reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth. When used in a mouthwash or toothpaste, lemongrass oil helps to freshen breath and reduce plaque and gum inflammation. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe mouth sores and gum infections that can lead to bad breath. Additionally, lemongrass oil has a pleasant citrus scent that can help mask unpleasant mouth odors. Regular use of lemongrass oil can help to keep the mouth clean and fresh, reducing the occurrence of bad breath.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils for bad breath, you'll need a few items like fresh or dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a clean glass jar, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a dark glass bottle for storage. Start by washing and drying the leaves thoroughly to remove any dirt or moisture. Then, chop the leaves finely and place them in the glass jar. Pour the carrier oil over the leaves, making sure they're completely covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it occasionally to help the oil extract from the leaves. After the extraction time, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle. Discard the solids and use the extracted oil as needed to help freshen breath and reduce bad breath. Keep in mind that this homemade oil is not as potent as commercial essential oils and should be used in moderation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oil, also known as ginger oil, is a natural remedy for bad breath. This oil is extracted from the roots of the ginger plant, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied to the mouth or gums, ginger oil helps to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause bad breath. Its antibacterial properties also help to kill the bacteria that are already present in the mouth, preventing them from multiplying and causing bad breath. Additionally, ginger oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and redness in the gums, which can also contribute to bad breath. As a result, ginger oil can help to freshen breath, reduce gum inflammation, and promote a healthy mouth.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils that help with bad breath, start by gathering fresh or dried Salvia officinalis leaves. You can grow them in your garden or purchase them from a local herbal store. Next, you'll need a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil, and a clean cheesecloth or coffee filter. Begin by placing the Salvia officinalis leaves in the glass jar, then pour the carrier oil over them, making sure they're completely covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it gently every few days. This process is called maceration, which helps to extract the beneficial properties from the herbs into the oil. After the maceration period, strain the oil through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Your homemade Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil is now ready to use. To help with bad breath, mix a few drops of the oil with water and use it as a mouthwash, or add it to your toothpaste for an antibacterial boost.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for bad breath

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oil, also known as Ceylon cinnamon oil, has several properties that make it effective in reducing bad breath. The oil contains compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which has antimicrobial properties that help kill bacteria in the mouth. These bacteria are often the main cause of bad breath, as they break down food particles and release unpleasant-smelling compounds. By eliminating these bacteria, Ceylon cinnamon oil can help reduce the production of volatile sulfur compounds, which are responsible for the strong odor associated with bad breath. Additionally, the oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe mouth sores and gum inflammation, further contributing to a fresher breath. When used in a dental rinse or mouthwash, Ceylon cinnamon oil can help combat bad breath and promote a healthier oral environment.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils that help with bad breath, you will need some fresh or dried sage leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass jar with a lid. Start by gathering a handful of fresh sage leaves and gently washing them with water to remove any dirt. Alternatively, you can use dried sage leaves if you don't have access to fresh ones. Next, place the sage leaves in the glass jar and pour enough carrier oil over them to completely cover the leaves. Make sure the jar is sealed tightly and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the oil extract from the leaves. This process is called maceration, and it allows the oil to penetrate deep into the leaves and release their medicinal properties. After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the sage leaves. Your Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil is now ready to use - simply add a few drops to your toothbrush or mouthwash to help freshen your breath and reduce bad breath caused by gum disease or other oral health issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum essential oil, also known as Ceylon cinnamon oil, is a natural remedy for bad breath. It has antibacterial properties that help kill the bacteria in the mouth, which cause bad breath. The oil's antimicrobial properties also help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, preventing infections that can cause bad breath. When used in a mouthwash or applied to the tongue and gums, Ceylon cinnamon oil can freshen breath and leave a pleasant, warm scent. Its deodorizing properties also help eliminate odors that can cause bad breath. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon oil contains compounds that have a numbing effect on the gums, reducing pain and discomfort associated with mouth infections and gum inflammation, further contributing to its effectiveness in combating bad breath.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil that helps with bad breath, you'll need some basic equipment and a few simple steps. First, harvest fresh leaves of the Salvia officinalis plant, also known as sage, and dry them completely in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator. Next, use a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil from the dried leaves. This process involves passing steam through the dried sage leaves, causing the oil to be released and collected in a separate container. The resulting essential oil will have a strong, earthy scent and contain compounds such as camphor and borneol, which are known for their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce bad breath. Once you've collected the essential oil, you can use it as a natural remedy for bad breath by adding a few drops to your toothpaste or mouthwash, or by inhaling it directly from a cloth or handkerchief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oil, also known as thyme oil, is a natural remedy for bad breath. The main reason is that it has antibacterial properties, which help to kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. These bacteria can grow and multiply when food particles and plaque are not cleaned properly, leading to bad breath. Thyme oil's antibacterial properties prevent the growth of these bacteria, reducing the cause of bad breath. Additionally, thyme oil has antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the growth of fungi and yeast in the mouth, further contributing to bad breath. When inhaled, the thyme oil can also help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can be a contributing factor to bad breath. Thyme oil's ability to kill bacteria and reduce inflammation makes it a popular natural remedy for bad breath.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils that can help with bad breath, you'll need fresh or dried leaves of the herb, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass jar with a lid. First, dry the Salvia officinalis leaves if they are fresh, by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper towel or clean cloth, allowing them to air dry for a few days. Next, combine a small amount of dried Salvia leaves with a carrier oil in the glass jar, making sure the leaves are completely covered by the oil. The general ratio is one part dried herb to three parts carrier oil, but you can adjust this to your liking. Now, seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every few days, allowing the oil to infuse and the flavors to meld together. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids, and your homemade Salvia officinalis oil is ready to use. You can apply a few drops of this oil to your gums or tongue to help freshen your breath and reduce the symptoms of bad breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum essential oil, also known as clove oil, has antibacterial properties that help kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. When these bacteria die, the bad smell they produce decreases. The oil also has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the gums, which can make it harder for bad breath to occur. Additionally, clove oil has a natural deodorizing effect that can help eliminate odors in the mouth. When applied to the teeth and gums, it can also help freshen breath and leave a pleasant, spicy scent. Its antimicrobial properties can also help reduce the growth of fungi and other microorganisms in the mouth that can contribute to bad breath.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils that help with bad breath, you'll need a few simple steps and some basic equipment. First, harvest fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and dry them thoroughly either by air-drying or using a food dehydrator. Next, chop the dried leaves into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar. Add a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil to the jar, making sure the leaves are completely covered. Then, seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. This process is called cold infusion and it will help to extract the beneficial properties of the Salvia officinalis into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Finally, your homemade Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil is ready to use as a natural remedy for bad breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, also known as tea tree oil, is a popular choice for bad breath. This is because it has antibacterial properties that help kill the bacteria in the mouth that can cause bad breath. When you inhale tea tree oil, it can help reduce the amount of bacteria in your mouth and kill the ones that cause bad breath. Tea tree oil also has antifungal properties, which can help prevent fungal infections in the mouth that can also cause bad breath. Furthermore, tea tree oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, which can also contribute to bad breath. When you use tea tree oil for bad breath, you can also expect a fresh and clean sensation in your mouth, which can boost your confidence.  The antibacterial properties of tea tree oil can also help prevent gum disease and other oral health issues that can cause bad breath. Tea tree oil is often used in dental products, such as mouthwashes and toothpaste, due to its effectiveness in reducing bad breath and promoting oral health. To use tea tree oil for bad breath, you can add a few drops to your toothpaste or mouthwash, or inhale it directly from a cloth or handkerchief.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oil, also known as sage oil, that helps with bad breath, you'll need dried Salvia officinalis leaves and a distillation apparatus. Start by cleaning and preparing the leaves, making sure they are free of any debris or contaminants. Next, place the leaves in the distillation apparatus, typically a still or a steam distiller, and heat the apparatus gently. As the heat increases, steam will rise through the leaves, releasing their essential oils. You'll need to collect the condensed steam, which will contain the sage oil, in a separate container. Allow the steam to cool and separate from the water, and then filter the resulting liquid to obtain the pure sage essential oil. This oil can be used in various ways, such as adding a few drops to your toothbrush or mouthwash to help combat bad breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oil is good for bad breath because it has antibacterial properties. This means it helps kill the germs in the mouth that can cause bad smell. The oil is extracted from the leaves of the sage plant, which has been used for centuries to freshen breath and improve oral health. When you use Salvia officinalis essential oil, it helps reduce the amount of bacteria in the mouth, making it a natural remedy for bad breath. It also has a deodorizing effect, which helps eliminate the unpleasant odors caused by bacteria. Additionally, the oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe mouth sores and reduce gum inflammation, further contributing to fresh breath. When used in combination with other oral care practices, Salvia officinalis essential oil can be a powerful tool in fighting bad breath.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils that help with bad breath, start by harvesting fresh leaves of the plant from a trusted supplier or from your garden. Make sure to identify the plant correctly and use only the leaves, as the flowers and stems may have different properties. Clean the leaves thoroughly with water and dry them completely to prevent any bacterial growth. Next, you'll need a steam distillation equipment, which can be a simple setup or a commercial distiller. Steam the leaves in the distiller to release their oils, and collect the steam as it condenses back into a liquid. This liquid is your essential oil, which you can then filter and store in a dark glass bottle. Once you have the oil, mix a few drops of it with water to create a mouthwash or mouth spray, and use it to freshen your breath. Note that it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal essential oils, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

intro_desc: Bad breath, also known as halitosis, can be caused by poor oral hygiene, gum disease, or even certain health conditions. It's essential to fix the issue because bad breath can affect our self-confidence, relationships, and even our overall well-being. Herbal essential oils can be a natural and effective way to combat bad breath. For instance, peppermint oil (Mentha x piperita) contains menthol, which has antibacterial properties that help kill the bacteria in our mouth that cause bad breath. Additionally, eucalyptus oil (Eucalyptus globulus) has decongestant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, making it easier to breathe and reducing bad breath. When we use these oils, they can also freshen our breath and leave a cooling sensation in our mouth. By fixing bad breath, we can improve our quality of life by feeling more confident in social situations, having better relationships with others, and simply feeling healthier and happier.
supplementary_best: A combination of 'Mentha x piperita' (peppermint) and 'Thymus vulgaris' (thyme) essential oils is effective in remedying bad breath. Peppermint oil contains menthol, which has antibacterial properties that help kill the bacteria causing bad breath. Thyme oil contains thymol, another antibacterial compound that targets and eliminates these bacteria. Additionally, peppermint oil's cooling sensation helps reduce inflammation in the mouth, further alleviating bad breath. Thyme oil's earthy and herbaceous scent complements peppermint oil's refreshing aroma, making this combination pleasant to use. This blend of peppermint and thyme oils is particularly effective because they both target the root cause of bad breath (bacteria) and work well together to create a synergistic effect, making them a popular choice for oral health.
supplementary_avoid: If you have bad breath, there are some herbal essential oils you should avoid using. Geranium essential oil (Pelargonium graveolens) might not be the best choice because it can cause mouth sores and irritation in some people, which can make bad breath worse. Bergamot essential oil (Citrus aurantium bergamia) contains bergapten, a compound that can cause photo-toxicity and increase sensitivity of the mouth and lips to sunlight, leading to further mouth problems. Ylang-ylang essential oil (Cananga odorata) has a strong scent that can be overpowering and may not be effective in masking bad breath. Lemon balm essential oil (Melissa officinalis) can cause allergic reactions and sensitivity in some people, and its sedative properties may not help with bad breath. Sage essential oil (Salvia officinalis alternative: Rosmarinus officinalis) may not be the best choice as it can be drying and irritating to the mouth, potentially making bad breath worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: Bad breath, also known as halitosis, can be caused by bacteria in the mouth, especially on the tongue and between the teeth. One natural remedy that can help is activated charcoal, which has been shown to be effective in absorbing odors and toxins. When taken as a powder or supplement, activated charcoal can help to neutralize the bacteria and reduce bad breath. Another remedy is to use a tongue scraper, which can remove bacteria and debris from the surface of the tongue, helping to prevent bad breath. Additionally, some people find that using a waterpik or oral irrigator can help to remove food particles and plaque from between the teeth, reducing the presence of bacteria that can cause bad breath. Also, improving oral hygiene by brushing and flossing regularly can help to reduce the presence of bacteria and prevent bad breath. Some people also find that eating foods high in fiber, such as fruits and vegetables, can help to stimulate saliva production, which can help to wash away bacteria and neutralize odors.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with bad breath, also known as halitosis, often experience other health issues. One common problem is gum disease or gingivitis, which can be caused by bacterial buildup in the mouth. The bacteria feed on food particles and multiply, leading to inflammation and infection of the gums. Medicinal herbs like neem and turmeric have antibacterial properties, which help reduce the growth of bacteria and inflammation. Neem, in particular, has been shown to reduce plaque and prevent gum disease.  Another issue associated with bad breath is dry mouth, also known as xerostomia. This occurs when the salivary glands do not produce enough saliva to keep the mouth moist. This can be caused by various factors, including medication, dehydration, or mouth breathing. The lack of saliva allows bacteria to multiply, leading to bad breath. Medicinal herbs like slippery elm and licorice root have demulcent properties, which help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth. They also stimulate saliva production, helping to keep the mouth moist.  Bad breath can also be a symptom of digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD). These conditions cause stomach acid to flow up into the esophagus, leading to inflammation and infection. Medicinal herbs like peppermint and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues. They also help stimulate digestion and reduce symptoms like bloating and nausea.  Furthermore, people with bad breath may experience sinusitis or respiratory infections. These conditions cause inflammation and infection of the sinuses and respiratory tract, leading to bad breath. Medicinal herbs like echinacea and goldenseal have immunomodulatory properties, which help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. They also have antibacterial properties, which help combat infections.  Finally, bad breath can be a symptom of diabetes or other metabolic disorders. These conditions cause high blood sugar levels, leading to inflammation and infection in the body. Medicinal herbs like cinnamon and berberine have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. They also help regulate blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for bad breath
